# VERIFY: #148 — Prepare-architecture play, three-axis profiling, quality standards
<!-- Generated by start-feature-planning | Plan sub-agent output -->

**Intent:** Verify that the three-axis profiling model, prepare-architecture play, quality standards, and prepare-implementation refactoring are correctly implemented, schema-compliant, and pipeline-coherent.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `quality-profile.md` exists at `core/components/memory/knowledge/product/quality-profile.md` with exactly 7 QP dimensions, each having a 5-level table (Level | Label | Description | Examples) matching the format in `product-profile.md` and `nfr-profile.md`
- [ ] `_index.md` references "Three-Axis Model" (not "Two-Axis"), lists all three profiles (PP, NFR, QP) in the contents table, and the flow diagram includes QP derivation after NFR
- [ ] `product.yaml` schema has a `profiles` section with `product_profile`, `nfr_profile`, and `quality_profile` sub-sections, each containing dimension entries with `id`, `level`, and optional `rationale`
- [ ] `draft-product-vision/SKILL.md` includes profile derivation logic: PP → NFR → QP sequential derivation, writes profiles to `product.yaml`
- [ ] `discover-product/reference/intent.yaml` has constraints requiring three-profile derivation, scenarios covering profile review, and failure conditions for missing profiles
- [ ] `discover-product/SKILL.md` is successfully rebaked via `/create-play --rebake discover-product` with profile derivation integrated into the workflow
- [ ] `scope-roadmap-epics/SKILL.md` references profiles from `product.yaml` for epic depth/priority reasoning
- [ ] `assess-feasibility/SKILL.md` references profiles from `product.yaml` for risk calibration
- [ ] `plan-roadmap/reference/intent.yaml` includes constraints for profile-informed epic scoping
- [ ] `plan-roadmap/SKILL.md` is successfully rebaked via `/create-play --rebake plan-roadmap`
- [ ] `quality-standards.yaml` schema exists with quality dimensions, thresholds, and measurement methods
- [ ] `architecture.yaml` schema has a `profiles_ref` field
- [ ] `draft-technical-approach/SKILL.md` accepts `profiles_ref` input and uses NFR + QP profiles in architecture decisions
- [ ] `prepare-architecture/reference/intent.yaml` exists with constraints requiring locked product.yaml + locked roadmap.yaml, producing architecture.yaml + quality-standards.yaml
- [ ] `prepare-architecture/SKILL.md` is successfully compiled via `/create-play prepare-architecture`
- [ ] `prepare-implementation/reference/intent.yaml` removes architecture scoping constraints and adds requirement for locked architecture.yaml as upstream input
- [ ] `prepare-implementation/SKILL.md` is successfully rebaked via `/create-play --rebake prepare-implementation`
- [ ] No existing fields in `product.yaml` schema are removed or renamed (backward compatibility)
- [ ] All new QP dimensions follow the "Profiles Are Knobs, Not Questions" principle (agent derives, user validates)
- [ ] `quality-profile.md` includes a "PP+NFR Guidance" section mapping profile conditions to QP defaults (matching pattern in `product-profile.md` NFR Guidance)

### Verification Steps

| Step | Method | Expected Outcome |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| V1. Validate quality-profile.md structure | Read file, count dimension sections (### QP-N headers), verify each has 5-level table | Exactly 7 dimensions (QP-1 through QP-7), each with 5 rows in the table, Level/Label/Description/Examples columns |
| V2. Validate _index.md Three-Axis Model | Read file, check for "Three-Axis Model" heading, verify contents table lists 3 entries | "Three-Axis Model" heading present, contents table lists product-profile.md, nfr-profile.md, quality-profile.md |
| V3. Validate product.yaml schema profiles | Read schema, verify `profiles` section with all three sub-sections | `profiles.product_profile`, `profiles.nfr_profile`, `profiles.quality_profile` all present with dimension entry structure |
| V4. Validate draft-product-vision skill | Read SKILL.md, check for profile derivation in Process section | Process includes steps for deriving PP, NFR (from PP), QP (from PP+NFR), writing to `profiles` section |
| V5. Validate discover-product intent.yaml | Read intent.yaml, check constraints and scenarios | New constraints for profile derivation (≥1), new scenarios covering profile review, failure condition for missing profiles |
| V6. Rebake discover-product | Run `/create-play --rebake discover-product` | SKILL.md compiles successfully, contains profile derivation workflow steps |
| V7. Validate scope-roadmap-epics profile usage | Read SKILL.md, grep for `profiles` or `product_profile` | Skill references profiles from product.yaml in its reasoning about epic depth |
| V8. Validate assess-feasibility profile usage | Read SKILL.md, grep for `profiles` or `nfr_profile` or `quality_profile` | Skill references profiles for risk calibration |
| V9. Validate plan-roadmap intent.yaml | Read intent.yaml, check for profile constraint | At least one constraint references profile-informed scoping |
| V10. Rebake plan-roadmap | Run `/create-play --rebake plan-roadmap` | SKILL.md compiles successfully |
| V11. Validate quality-standards.yaml schema | Read schema file, verify structure | Schema has dimensions, thresholds, measurement_methods, profiles_ref fields |
| V12. Validate architecture.yaml schema profiles_ref | Read schema, check for `profiles_ref` field | `profiles_ref` field present with description pointing to product.yaml profiles |
| V13. Validate draft-technical-approach skill | Read SKILL.md, check for profiles_ref input | Skill accepts profiles_ref, uses NFR+QP in architecture decisions |
| V14. Validate prepare-architecture intent.yaml | Read intent.yaml, check constraints and pre-conditions | Requires locked product.yaml, locked roadmap.yaml; produces architecture.yaml, quality-standards.yaml |
| V15. Compile prepare-architecture | Run `/create-play prepare-architecture` | SKILL.md compiles successfully with prepare-architecture workflow |
| V16. Validate prepare-implementation refactoring | Read intent.yaml, check that architecture scoping is removed | No constraints about drafting architecture.yaml; new constraint requiring locked architecture.yaml input |
| V17. Rebake prepare-implementation | Run `/create-play --rebake prepare-implementation` | SKILL.md compiles successfully, no architecture drafting steps remain |
| V18. Backward compatibility check | Compare old vs new product.yaml schema | All existing fields preserved; `profiles` section is purely additive |
| V19. Pipeline coherence check | Trace data flow across plays | `discover-product` → product.yaml (with profiles) → `plan-roadmap` → roadmap.yaml → `prepare-architecture` → architecture.yaml + quality-standards.yaml → `prepare-implementation` (reads locked architecture.yaml) |

### Edge Cases

| Scenario | Expected Behavior |
|----------|------------------|
| Existing locked product.yaml without profiles section | Downstream plays (plan-roadmap, prepare-architecture) degrade gracefully — either prompt for profile derivation or use sensible defaults. No hard crash. |
| Library-type product (type: "library") | QP derivation still runs but some dimensions may default to lower levels. NFR Guidance and QP Guidance handle library context. Profile section is still written to product.yaml. |
| Product with PP-6 = 1 (POC) | Per existing NFR Guidance, all NFR dimensions default to 1. QP dimensions should similarly have low defaults for POC-level delivery ambition. |
| prepare-architecture invoked without locked roadmap.yaml | Pre-flight check halts immediately with clear error — "roadmap.yaml must be LOCKED before architecture planning begins." |
| prepare-implementation invoked without locked architecture.yaml | Pre-flight check halts immediately — "architecture.yaml must be LOCKED. Run /prepare-architecture first." |
| QP dimension level set to 5 for all dimensions | All thresholds in quality-standards.yaml are at maximum stringency — valid but extreme. No overflow or schema violation. |
| Rebake of discover-product when intent.yaml has invalid YAML | `/create-play --rebake` fails gracefully with YAML parse error, does not corrupt existing SKILL.md. |
| prepare-architecture invoked for product with no agentic components | `agentic` section in architecture.yaml is omitted per existing draft-technical-approach behavior. quality-standards.yaml still produced. |
| Plan-roadmap consuming profiles for epic scoping with all profiles at level 1 | Epics are scoped with minimal depth — MVP-appropriate. No error from low profile values. |
